name: lcx-contribute-bug-fix
tagline: Debugs a LazyCodex/Codex bug and delivers it as a fork PR (upstream openai/codex) or a verified-fix issue (code-yeongyu/lazycodex) via gh/gi
verdict: Scoped to two named GitHub repos, uses only ambient gh auth, no secret reads, no obfuscation or hidden instructions. Main risk: it can autonomously push a branch, open a public PR against openai/codex, or file a public issue under the user's identity with no explicit pre-submission confirmation gate. Also lacks a declared-tooling section and any version/chan

highlights.summary: Steps 12-13 push a branch, open a PR against `openai/codex`, or file a public issue on `code-yeongyu/lazycodex` with no explicit final confirmation step. 'Stop Conditions' only cover ambiguous target

No explicit 'do not use when' statement; the only negative-trigger signal is an implicit contrast with the separate `$lcx-report-bug` skill for reporting without a fix, never spelled out in this file.
No upfront 'Requirements' section declaring `gh` (authenticated), `git`, and `node` as prerequisites — tooling needs only surface implicitly inside the step-by-step bash blocks.
